frontiers.title: Sandy Chaos Proof Frontier V1
frontiers.objective: Force the major Sandy Chaos surfaces through a common proof-path ladder, keep one ranked frontier, and point the next proof move at the strongest discriminating candidate.
frontiers.currentState: `/home/ian/projects/sandy-chaos/plans/today_frontier_2026-04-18.md` retired the old `SC-CONCEPT-0006` active slot and reranked the frontier to `SC-CONCEPT-0003` hyperstition-policy-attractor-dynamics. That lane has now completed the bounded proof package it was owed: `plans/hyperstition_level4_null_model_comparison_v0.md` established separation from a null baseline, `plans/hyperstition_level4_ablation_comparison_v0.md` showed the win survives stronger partial baselines, `plans/hyperstition_level4_result_packet_v0.md` compressed the result into one bounded Level-4 packet, and `plans/hyperstition_level4_robustness_pass_v0.md` confirmed that Arm A's bidirectional corridor structure survives ±10% one-at-a-time drift across all five non-noise parameters. The live pressure is no longer stability. It is whether a stronger comparator with temporal asymmetry intact can reproduce the self-defeating corridor without the full narrative-conditioned feedback loop.
frontiers.nextAction: Design one stronger comparator for `SC-CONCEPT-0003` that preserves `temporal_bias_gain > 0` while breaking the narrative-conditioned feedback loop differently than the current no-temporal-bias ablation, then test whether the bidirectional corridor split still requires the full Arm A mechanism. Do not reopen symbolic-maps hardening or broaden into schema/theory expansion unless this comparator materially weakens the structural-specificity claim.
frontiers.selectionReason: Current active Ygg baton in assistant-home, corrected to the post-2026-04-18 frontier rerank.

frontiers.nextAction: Use the three cues in live work, notice which feel natural vs annoying, and capture 2-3 real fragments before deciding what should harden.

frontiers.title: Sandy Chaos Alignment Constraints V1
frontiers.objective: Translate the strongest Sandy Chaos ideals into concrete Ygg design constraints so Ygg evolves in sync with the host project rather than drifting into a merely clever control shell.
frontiers.currentState:
Completed the first compact constraint packet in `config/SANDY_CHAOS_ALIGNMENT_CONSTRAINT_PACKET_V1.md`, seeded the first advisory `state/concept-spine.json` surface, wired the `work` front door to consume baton state plus `state/active-work.json` plus `state/concept-spine.json`, and implemented Dispatch Phase 2a from `config/YGG_DISPATCH_PHASE2A_MINISPEC.md`. The limiting reagent then shifted from dispatch polish to proof-governance compression, so the next pass produced a durable Sandy Chaos proof-path ladder, a ranked frontier note, a new Sandy Chaos spine concept (`SC-CONCEPT-0008`), and a pressure event that selected the next proof surface.

Completion note: alignment constraints work successfully handed off into the Sandy Chaos proof-frontier lane.
